qas.q: Off base dispatches and/or responses that are categorized as "non-pursuit" will be approved by who?
qas.a: - 7th Bomb Wing Commander

qas.q: SF does NOT have off base jurisdiction. When considering the exterior of the base, SF personnel may only respond to what kind of incidents? Directed by who?
qas.a:
- Traffic accidents/incidents
- As directed by the Defense Force Commander or higher authority

qas.q: When is the only time emergency lights are permitted for off base incidents?
qas.a: - When safety precautions are necessary

qas.q: Who has the authority to authorize off-base responses when a patrol is in "fresh pursuit"?
qas.a: - Flight Chief

qas.q: The FC can authorize the off-base pursuit of an individual under what circumstances?
qas.a: - If the individual is suspected to have committed a serious crime and cannot be stopped by gate closure

qas.q: In any case where there is an off-base "fresh pursuit", who must BDOC immediately notify?
qas.a: - Both the Defense Force Commander and the 7th Missions Support Group Commander

qas.q: During off base pursuits, what will patrols make sure they are not utilizing?
qas.a: - Lights and sirens

qas.q: During off base pursuits, what will patrols avoid doing?
qas.a: - They will avoid overcrowding the suspect's vehicle to prevent them from increasing in speed or reckless driving

qas.q: FCs will ensure all pursuit of vehicles/personnel and traffic stops is conducted IAW what? (3)
qas.a: - AFI 31-118( to include Ch.4), AFI 31-218 and AFMAN 31-201V3

qas.q: All funds escorts will be performed IAW what?
qas.a: - 7th Bomb Wing Integrated Defense/Anti-Terrorism Plan (ID/AT)

qas.q: A SF escort is required for government agencies transporting funds exceeding what dollar amount?
qas.a:
- $25,000 or more.

*Note: during higher FPCONS, escorts may be used for an even lower amount

qas.q: When dispatched to provide a funds escort, what are the responsibilities of the patrol upon arrival and termination?
qas.a:
1.) Notify BDOC when on scene
2.) Meet custodian inside facility and walk with them to their transport vehicle
3.) Ensure route and procedures prior to movement
4.) Follow them to destination
5.) Upon arrival, walk custodian inside, do not depart until funds are secured
6.) Notify BDOC/ECC of completion

qas.q: For non-government funds, who will perform the escort function?
qas.a: - Owner/user

qas.q: What are the 15 Security Forces Response Codes/Brevity Codes?
qas.a: - Landlines

qas.q: What is considered an alternate form of communication for all SF personnel?
qas.a:
- Landline Operations
- Discrepancies will be reported to the 7th Communications Squadron

qas.q: Other than duress check, all static posts will conduct a test of what upon assuming post if available? Who will discrepancies be reported to?
qas.a: - Devices that have photo or video capability

qas.q: What type of devices are not authorized in restricted areas (by SF) unless being used for official purposes?
qas.a: - Manual signal techniques (hand/arm signals, vocal signals)

qas.q: What is used as a third means of communication if radio and landline capability is not an option?
qas.a: - Waving of headgear (helmet, hat, cap) in a circular motion with arm above head shouting "COVERED WAGON"

qas.q: Describe the primary "manual signal" for indicating a COVERED WAGON?
qas.a: - Short and long flashlight beams or short and long whistle blasts

qas.q: Describe the alternate "manual signal" for indicating a COVERED WAGON?
qas.a: - Hold both arms out horizontally like a T while shouting help or relief

qas.q: Describe the primary "manual signal" for HELP or RELIEF?
qas.a: - Use a constant flashlight beam pointed out in front of the body moving it up and down vertically

qas.q: Describe the alternate "manual signal" for HELP or RELIEF?
qas.a: - Firing 3 shots in rapid succession straight up into the air away from personnel and resources.

qas.q: Emergency discharge of a firearm for communication purposes will only be used as a last resort when all other means of communication have failed and an emergency exists. In such a situation, how will you signal?
qas.a: 50 feet

qas.q: Radio transmissions will not be made within _______ feet of an aircraft undergoing fuel cell maintenance or being refueled/de-fueled.
qas.a:
- 25 feet
- 100 feet

qas.q: Unless otherwise directed by EOD/Fire Chief, Handheld cell phone/radio transmissions will not be made within ______ for handheld and ______ for mobile
qas.a:
- Channel #1A
- Channel #2A

qas.q: What channels will be used routinely by on duty posts and patrols as the primary SF net? Which will serve as secondary?
qas.a:
- Zone B
- FC or higher

qas.q: All channels on which zone (radio) are authorized for training and exercises? Who must approve?
qas.a: - Threats, violence, constraints, or other actions brought to bear on someone to do something against their will

qas.q: What is Duress?
Primary, alternate and exercise duress words will be established IAW what AFI?
qas.a: - The use of an actual duress word, mis-authentication, activation of a duress alarm

qas.q: What are signs of positive duress?
qas.a: - Non-compliance with established procedures or failure to answer radio transmissions

qas.q: What are signs of passive duress?
qas.a: - SF Authenticator

qas.q: What is used to verify the security status of on-duty personnel or to validate radio transmissions?
qas.a: - Positive duress

qas.q: For what situation will the SF authenticator NOT be used to verify status?
qas.a: - By a rotating amber light attached to the VCC facing the gate facility

qas.q: How will Arnold Gate be alerted of a BDOC duress?
qas.a: - Arnold Gate

qas.q: Who will take control of the radio net until the alternate BDOC is activated for BDOC duress/relocation?
qas.a: - By a rotating red light attached to the VCC facing the gate facility

qas.q: How will Arnold Gate be alerted of a duress at the VCC?
qas.a: - Security 1 since it's a restricted area. Responding patrols will establish a cordon within the facility

qas.q: Who will respond for duress at the 7th Bomb Wing Command Post?
qas.a: - COVERED WAGON

qas.q: For hostile indications at the 7th BW/CP, what will be implemented?
qas.a: - Fire Department

qas.q: Who can we contact to assist with opening the 7th BW/CP door in a response?
qas.a: - AA&E Alarm Activation procedures

qas.q: In the event of duress at the armory, BDOC will initiate which procedure?
qas.a: - CATM

qas.q: Where is the alternate armory located?
qas.a: - Conduct stand-off weapon attacks

qas.q: Clandestine areas are used to monitor likely avenues of approach to the installation. These are areas where adversaries could possibly do what?
qas.a: -Area #1

qas.q: ________________ is the drainage ditch area north of the transient ramp running east to west, from the flight line fence to Taxiway A and the hill north of the drainage ditch.
qas.a: - Area #2

qas.q: _______________ is the west side of the active runway from north to south and west to the fence line.
qas.a: - Area #3

qas.q: _______________ is the drainage ditch bordering the south end of the runway along the perimeter to the compound at the south end of 3rd Street.
qas.a: - Area #4

qas.q: ________________ is the drainage ditch east of Marion Rd adjacent to blast shield # 4 ( Higher Headquarter (HHQ) ramp) to the intersection of Avenue E-1 and 2nd Street.
qas.a: - Area #5

qas.q: ________________ is the Maryland Gate area of base housing.
qas.a: - Area #6

qas.q: ________________ is the Delaware Gate area of base housing.
qas.a: - Area #7

qas.q: ________________ is the Northwest perimeter adjacent to the Memorial Center.
Where can we retrieve the keys to the armory in case of duress situation and we cannot verify status?
qas.a: - 7th BW/CC, 7th MSG/CC, 7th SFS/DFC and WCP

qas.q: Pyramid Alerting Procedures or "Recalls" can be initiated by who? (4)
qas.a:
- 2300 to 0500
- IAW with the Youth Supervision Policy

qas.q: What is the curfew for dependents/civilians under the age of 18? Under what policy?
qas.a: - Remove all individuals from vehicle (if applies), evacuate the immediate area, engage barriers, establish cordon, redirect traffic and contact BDOC for further instructions

qas.q: When encountering a possible bomb threat, what are some things you must ensure you do?
qas.a: - 7th BW ID/AT Plan, the AF Form 440, Bomb Threat Aid and Applicable QRCs

qas.q: In the case of a bomb threat, you must follow the procedures outlined in what? (4)
qas.a: - Hostage/barricaded subject checklist and maintain security of the facility until backup arrives

qas.q: In the case where BDOC can not obtain proper authentication from a manned facility personnel, the FC will direct the implementation of what situation?
qas.a: - B-1 = front only, while C-130 = front and rear

qas.q: If BDOC confirms suspicious aircraft engine start via 7th/317th, security 2 and 3 will be directed to block the aircraft. How will B-1 aircraft be blocked as opposed to C-130?
qas.a: - set hand brake, turn off engine, turn on all lights and deploy to a safe location while keeping aircraft insight

qas.q: During an unauthorized engine start, security 2 and 3 will block the suspicious aircraft. One vehicle is in place what must they do to ensure their safety?
qas.a: - point at individual with one hand and with the other move hand from left to right giving the "cut it" signal

qas.q: The first SF member to establish positive visual contact with unauthorized person inside the cockpit will give them the sign to shut down the engine? How will they give the signal?
qas.a: - by firing at the tires or any other vital components as directed by the IC.

qas.q: During an unauthorized engine start, if the aircraft operator fails to comply, how will SF go about disabling the aircraft?
qas.a: - Security 1, 2 and 3 will go mobile in their assigned areas

qas.q: Power outages that affect restricted areas will be up channeled to BDOC with a SALUTE report. How will security units posture themselves until power is restored?
qas.a:
- Every Sunday night
- Using the SF Form 4 MPA Lighting Chart

qas.q: How often will security 1 conduct a restricted area lighting check? Utilizing what?
qas.a: - The WCP or higher

qas.q: Who will COVERED WAGON reports be up channeled to?
qas.a: - BDOC —> DFC —> Installation CC —> AFGSC Command Post

qas.q: If the WCP is under duress, how will a COVERED WAGON be reported?
BDOC camera surveillance system is comprised of how many cameras throughout the installation?
qas.a: - Flight Chief

qas.q: Who will respond to all security incidents, COVERED WAGON, and IFE situations?
qas.a: - Installation CC or designated representative

qas.q: RIEVCs are randomized "checks" directed and authorized by who?
qas.a: - 🚫Search and inspection

qas.q: Which two term should never be used in conjunction with RIEVCs?
qas.a: - To prevent unauthorized removal of government property or classified information

qas.q: What is the purpose of a RIEVC?
qas.a: - Detain vehicle occupants, secure vehicle, contact SJA and the information security office for further guidance

qas.q: If an individual is transporting classified without an authorization letter, SF must what?
qas.a:
- Proprietary
- with the exception of restricted areas which fall under military jurisdiction

qas.q: Dyess AFB is covered by what form of jurisdiction? With the exception of what?
qas.a: - from the perimeter fence outward 30 feet to include roadways immediately surrounding the installation

qas.q: Dyess AFB's jurisdiction/legal boundary extends how far?
qas.a: - Less than Lethal

qas.q: Active barrier engagement is considered to be what kind of force?
qas.a: - to prevent an unauthorized vehicle from entering an installation

qas.q: What is the purpose of vehicle barriers?
qas.a: - To locate the subject

qas.q: Upon notification of a gate runner, what is the is the responsibility of the primary installation patrol?
qas.a: - To prevent access to the flight lineup

qas.q: Upon notification of a gate runner, what is the responsibility of the primary security patrol?
qas.a: - Will begin closing flight line gates from North to South

qas.q: If there is a gate runner at Tye Gate, flight line security units will do what?
qas.a: - To determine if the subject is responsible/legitimate or if it's a suspicious person whose presents warrants further inquiry

qas.q: What are the primary purposes (2) for conducting a field interview?
qas.a: - Federal Privacy Act of 1974

qas.q: Where does it state that the SF member conducting a field interview must inform the individual that a record of the interview will be kept on file?
qas.a:
- AF Form 3907
- BDOC and then to S3O

qas.q: All field interviews will be completed on what form? Forwarded to who for review?
qas.a: - Field Interview Card file located in BDOC

qas.q: Where will all field interview forms be maintained?
qas.a: - OPERATION WRONG TURN

qas.q: What is used to describe an individual who arrives at the gate with no military affiliation nor reason for entry?
qas.a: - Intoxication of the Driver, Actions of the Driver and Place

qas.q: What are the three ELEMENTS for a DWI offense?
qas.a: - Vehicle in Motion, Personal Contact and Pre-Apprehension Screening

qas.q: What are the three basic phases of DWI detection?
qas.a: - Blood Alcohol Concentration levels (BAC)

qas.q: What information will be used to to determine either intoxication or impairment of an individual suspected of DWI?
qas.a:
- Age 21
- 6 months

qas.q: Personnel under ______ found with any trace of alcohol may be punished under the UCMJ and have driving privileges revoked for up to _____________.
qas.a: - The present capability and power to dominate, direct, and regulate motor vehicles; regardless if the motor vehicle is operational.

qas.q: For personnel subject to the UCMJ, "actual physical control" defines what when concerning DWIs?
qas.a: - In actual physical control

qas.q: "Actions of the Driver" are identified as the subject was driving, operating or ________________________________ of a vehicle.
qas.a: - True

qas.q: True/False: "actual physical control" consists of being in a motor vehicle with the motor running or simply being asleep in the back seat.
qas.a:
- SF will ensure the suspect does not exit the vehicle
- Because APD must physically see the suspect behind the wheel of the vehicle to make an arrest

qas.q: If a civilian is stopped for DWI, what will SF personnel ensure does not happen? Why?
qas.a: - They May ask the driver to place vehicle in park and remove the keys from the ignition

qas.q: When a civilian is expected of a DWI, what may the SF member in contact with that suspect request for their safety?
qas.a: - The offense as being located within the legal boundaries of DAFB

qas.q: DWI Operations: What does PLACE define?
qas.a: - liquor bottles, beer cans, or pill containers

qas.q: What are some examples of items found in or around a suspect vehicle in plain view that may be used as evidence and to establish probable cause?
qas.a: - Starting is when SF member first notices the vehicle and ending is when the individual is released from custody

qas.q: What is the starting and ending points for the process of gathering evidence for DWI?
qas.a: - Vehicle in Motion, Personal Contact, and Pre-Apprehension Screening

qas.q: What are the three basic phases to DWI detection?
qas.a: - Vehicle in Motion

qas.q: During which of the three phases of DWI may an SF member witness abnormal driving behavior to include normal traffic offenses?
qas.a: - Starts when an SF member first notices a vehicle and it ends when an SF member exits the patrol car or stops the suspects vehicle at the gate

qas.q: What are the starting and ending points for the "Vehicle in Motion" phase?
qas.a: - Starts when the SF member approaches the suspects vehicle and ends when the SF member decides to have the driver exit the vehicle

qas.q: What are the starting and ending points for the "Personal Contact" phase?
qas.a: - Inability to follow instructions, bloodshot eyes, alcohol odor, fumbling for credentials, or producing wrong credentials

qas.q: What are some good indicators that a driver is under the influence of alcohol?
qas.a: - Starts when the individual exits the vehicle and ends when the SF member decides to release or apprehend/detain the driver

qas.q: What are the starting and ending points for the "Pre-Apprehension Screening" phase?
qas.a: - DD FM 1920, Alcoholic Influence Report

qas.q: All observations of the Standard Field Sobriety Test will be recorded on what form?
qas.a: - If they are sick or taking any medications

qas.q: Before administering the SFST on a suspect, what must you ask the individual?
qas.a: - Safe, dry, flat, well lit area out of the way of traffic. No surrounding bystanders and make sure emergency lights are off

qas.q: Describe the ideal situation/environment for administering the SFST on a suspect?
qas.a: - Horizontal Gaze Nystagmus (HGN), 9-Step Walk & Turn Test, and One Leg Stand Test

qas.q: The Standard Field Sobriety Tests (3) utilized by certified 7 SFS personnel are:
qas.a: - 7 LRS

qas.q: If an SF member has to apprehend a suspect for DWI, who will be contacted to tow the vehicle (at the owner's risk) if the suspect does not allow anyone to move it for him?
qas.a: - Blood, Breath, or Urine

qas.q: SF will give the Implied Consent Policy prior to any testing of what?
qas.a: - 1 Year

qas.q: Refusal to submit to a test under the Implied Consent Policy will result in revocation of driving privileges for a minimum of_______________.
qas.a: - False

qas.q: True/False: Under the Implied Consent Policy Consent Policy, a suspect has a right to consult an attorney before they make a decision on agreeing to a blood test
qas.a:
- Blood test
- 7 MDG

qas.q: What is the only type of test used at DAFB for the purpose of detecting alcohol levels within a suspect? Who conducts the test?
qas.a: - Monday mornings between 0001-0400

qas.q: When will the FC conduct barrier testing for both Arnold Gate and Tye Gate?
qas.a: - SF E-5 or above

qas.q: What rank do you have to be to authenticate crew orders for transient aircraft?
qas.a: - A Temporary Restricted Area

qas.q: What will be established for all transient aircraft parked outside a restricted area?
qas.a: - To determine if the person(s) being questioned may or may not have information of official interest to the interviewer

qas.q: What is the overall purpose of conducting interviews?
qas.a: - Custodial and Non-Custodial Interviews

qas.q: What are the two types of interviews that a SF member will perform?
qas.a: - Advise them of their Article 31 Rights

qas.q: Regardless of whether or not they are in custody, what must you do before interviewing a military member suspected of committing an offense?
qas.a: - Their Miranda Rights

qas.q: In a custodial interview, civilians must be advised of what prior to questioning?
qas.a: - Reasonable belief that a crime was committed by the person being apprehended, crime was committed within the jurisdiction of DAFB

qas.q: What are the probable causes to apprehend a military member?
qas.a: - Clearly notifies a subject that they are under apprehension

qas.q: An Apprehension occurs when an SF member does what?
qas.a: - Custody

qas.q: The restraint of free movement of a suspect defines what?
qas.a: - SJA

qas.q: In instances involving the detaining of a civilian suspect, who will be called for guidance whenever APD cannot/will not respond?
qas.a: - Juveniles

qas.q: Individuals under the age of 18 are defined as what?
qas.a: - Parent, guardian, sponsor. If none of these are available ensure that there is a second patrol

qas.q: When detaining juveniles, it is ideal to have who present to witness all actions?
qas.a: - Two SF members

qas.q: How many SF members are required to be in the vehicle whenever transporting Juveniles?
qas.a: - A violation of catogorized felony laws subject to state and federal

qas.q: What is categorized as a Major Incident?
qas.a: - Terry vs Ohio

qas.q: In which U.S Supreme Court case was it ruled that the 4th amendment permits law enforcement to stop, detain, frisk a person suspected of criminal activity without their consent? (Terry Stop)
qas.a: - Reasonable Suspicion or Probable Cause

qas.q: Patrols may only search an individual if they have a _______________________________ or _______________________________ to suspect criminal activity has occurred or is about to occur.
qas.a: - Evidence storage lockers located in Alt BDOC

qas.q: Where will evidence be stored?
qas.a: - S2i

qas.q: Who will be notified when items are too large to fit inside the evidence lockers located at the Alt BDOC?
qas.a: - SF Investigator or AFOSI

qas.q: Fire arms or ammunition will be stored in the evidence locker located at the SF armory. Who are the only individuals that these materials will be released to?
qas.a: - Drugs/paraphernalia, weapons/ammunition, explosives

qas.q: All evidence will be stored at the Alt BDOC with the exception of what?
BDOC may revive found property if the replacement value is greater than _____________.
qas.a: - AF FM 1364, Consent for Search and Seizure

qas.q: On what Form should Consent to Search be written?
qas.a:
- Member requesting authority
- SJA and Base Magistrate

qas.q: Who will conduct a 3 way call to obtain authority to search and seize? To whom?
qas.a: - AF FM 1176

qas.q: After obtain authority to search and seize via 3 way call to SJA and Base Magistrate, what form will be completed?
qas.a: - AF FM 3226

qas.q: What form will be utilized for authority to apprehend and in private dwelling resident?
qas.a: - Military Family Housing

qas.q: What is considered to be a private dwelling?
qas.a: - 7 BW/CC

qas.q: Who is the termination authority for COVERED WAGONS?
qas.a: - When a probable or actual hostile act against protection level 1, 2 or 3 resources has been detected

qas.q: What is the definition of a COVERED WAGON?
qas.a: - When custodian arrives on scene

qas.q: At what point will it be ok for SF to conduct an interior check of an alarmed facility?
qas.a: - Alarm Priority Listing

qas.q: What is used to prioritize alarms whenever there are simultaneous alarm activations?
qas.a: - ESS/NCO

qas.q: Who maintains the Alarm Priority Listing and controls the distribution?
qas.a: - AA&E facilities and Funds Facilities

qas.q: BDOC will direct all gates to close for alarm activations involving what facilities?
qas.a: - 10 Minutes

qas.q: Patrol response times to AA&E alarm activations will not exceed _________________.
qas.a: - On official business

qas.q: Personnel requesting entry into Dyess AFB must present one of the following credentials
qas.a: - Political Asylum

qas.q: Local, State, and Federal Law Enforcement officers may be granted access into Dyess AFB so long as they are what?
qas.a: - That a base official is in route to speak with them

qas.q: _________________________________ is when an individual is unwilling or unable to return to their home country because of fear of persecution on account of race, religion, nationality, membership in a particular social group, or political opinion.
qas.a: - SEVEN BRIDGES

qas.q: What will IECs inform individuals requesting Political Asylum of while they wait?
qas.a: - Place vehicle in park, remove keys from the ignition, and provide a valid form of identification

qas.q: If an individual requests Political Asylum, what code word will the IEC up-channel?
qas.a: - Rough sketch and Finished sketch

qas.q: When encountered with a possible DWI, at a minimum, have the driver do what?
qas.a: - Record the date, time, location, and weather conditions

qas.q: What are the type types of sketches that can be made of a crime scene?
qas.a:
- At least once per quarter
- By a Certified BDOC controller or FC

qas.q: Upon arrival at a crime scene, what are some protective actions that the responding patrol must take?
qas.a: - SJA

qas.q: How often will the Alt BDOC be activated to ensure readiness? By who?
qas.a: - Security 2

qas.q: Who will be notified of all shoplifting cases involving civilians?
qas.a: - Security 1

qas.q: "Internal Security Response Team (ISRT) serves as the B-1 mass parking restricted area and maintenance/aircraft hangars and maintains a three minute response time to alarms within the restricted areas." It also is posted in the restricted area on Patrol Sector 2. Which Security Team does this describe?
qas.a: - Evidence of a crime or there is a suspect to be processed

qas.q: "External Security Response Team (ESRT) serves as the flight line supervisor and maintains a five minute response time to alarms within the restricted areas." describes which Security Team?
qas.a: - A Notification of Privileges Termination Letter

qas.q: SF will only respond to shopliftings when there is what?
qas.a: - Security 3

qas.q: What will be issued to all shoplifters upon approval from the base magistrate?
qas.a: - Sponsor's unit CC/first sergeant and base family advocacy officer

qas.q: "Internal Security Response Team (ISRT) serves as the C-130J mass parking restricted area and maintenance/aircraft hangars and maintains a three minute response time to alarms within the restricted areas." It is posted in the restricted area in Patrol Sector 3. Which Security Team is this?
qas.a: - Minimum of two

qas.q: Who is to be notified of any responses involving domestic violence?
qas.a: - AF Form 1314

qas.q: How many patrols will be dispatched for domestic violence reports?
qas.a:
- VCC
- Armory

qas.q: AF Form __________ is the Firearms Registration Roster.
qas.a: - Custodian name, estimated time of arrival and description

qas.q: The Firearms Registration Roster is located at the ______________ for housing residents and the ________________ for dorm residents.
qas.a: - At least two SF members will conduct an interior check. Use an On duty MWD if possible. Once check is complete, set up crime scene and contact custodian, OSI, and S2I

qas.q: What will BDOC ensure that the on-scene SF member is aware of prior to a building custodian's arrival during an alarm response?
qas.a: - 15 minutes

qas.q: If exterior check reveals signs of forced entry at an alarmed facility, what will be the actions of responding patrols?
BDOC/ECC will initiate passive duress procedures if the termination notice for funds escort is not received within ______ minutes of estimated ETA.
qas.a: - Controlled Area

qas.q: Funds that are ______________ or more, require at least one armed security forces member.
qas.a: - 25 feet

qas.q: Air Force Accounting and Finance offices and funds facilities handling or storing $100,000 or more of government funds will be designated as a what?
qas.a: - 100 feet

qas.q: When responding to a bomb threat, SF will NOT utilize a hand held radio within how many feet of the suspicious package?
qas.a: - 500 feet and upwind

qas.q: When responding to a bomb threat, SF will NOT utilize a vehicle mounted radio within how many feet of the suspicious package?
qas.a: - The size of the package

qas.q: What is the initial cordon size for a bomb threat?
qas.a:
- 30 minutes
- 30 minutes

qas.q: What will determine the "immediate area" on the scene of a possible bomb?
qas.a: - Proprietary

qas.q: When responding to a bomb threat, ensure no personnel are permitted into the affected area ______ minutes prior to and ______ 30 minutes after detonation time (if detonation time is known).
qas.a:
Sector 1 - Includes all areas East of 3rd Street

Sector 2 - Includes all areas West of 3rd Street and South of Avenue C

Sector 3 - Includes all areas West of 3rd Street and North of Avenue C.

qas.q: Security forces exercise what kind of jurisdiction over all personnel on the installation subject to the UCMJ?
qas.a: - 5 minute

qas.q: Dyess AFB is divided in to 3 installation patrol sectors. What are they?
qas.a: Security 1

qas.q: One external security response team (ESRT) will maintain a _____ minute response time to alarms in the restricted area.
qas.a: - 3 minutes

qas.q: Who serves as the flight line supervisor, but can be dispatched to other areas on the base as directed by S3O or higher?
qas.a: - Aircraft Mass Parking Areas (MPA), all Aircraft, 7 BW/CP (bldg 8030), and Alternate CP when activated

qas.q: How fast must the Internal Response Teams (ISRT) respond to the B-1 and C-130 mass parking restricted areas and air craft hangers?
qas.a: - Restriced Area Badge (RAB)

qas.q: What locations on Dyess AFB are designated restricted areas?
qas.a: - DD Form 2 series, CAC card, AF Form 354

qas.q: What is the AF Form 1199D?
qas.a: Yes, so long as they are listed on an EAL and posses a home station RAB

qas.q: Other than your RAB, what are three other kinds of Armed Forces identification you may need to enter a restricted area?
Can a person TDY to Dyess AFB be authorized onto a restricted area?
qas.a: - Victor Row, Romeo Row, Spot A5, Spot A11, Spot A17 and Spot B17

qas.q: How many ECPs are there that allow entry to the restricted area on the flight line?
qas.a: - Flight Chief

qas.q: Name the 6 entry control points (ECPs) that allow entry into the flight lines restricted areas:
qas.a: - Time/date of tour, destination, number of personnel and their info, and if photography is authorized

qas.q: Who is the termination authority for flight line security incidents?
qas.a: - One check every 4 hours

qas.q: All flight line tours and visits will be pre-announced to the MOC and BDOC. The notification will include what information?
qas.a: - 8 minutes

qas.q: Transient aircraft. SF will secure all transient aircraft parked outside of the restricted area with ropes and stanchions. How often will checks be conducted?
qas.a: - NAOC

qas.q: NAOC alert aircrew and billets (building 7407) will be designated a controlled area when occupied by NAOC crew members. What is the ESRTs response time for this facility?
qas.a:
- AA&E and funds facilities
- upon termination of alarm activation

qas.q: Response to _______________ takes precedence over all other ESRT duties.
qas.a: - Barriers, Gates, and Security Structures

qas.q: Explain the process for unannounced aircraft arrival:
qas.a: - Once per shift

qas.q: All gates must close when alarms are received from what type of facilities? When can they open?
qas.a: - Establish an inner security zone, block off any escape routes and exits. Evacuate non-essential personal. Establish an outer security zone, set up cordon and ECP outside of weapons range if possible.

qas.q: What does the ESRTs physical security check consist of?
qas.a: - Nonspecific threat of a terrorist attack or hostile act

qas.q: How often will the ESRT/ESRTs conduct a physical security check?
qas.a: - When a terrorist attack is likely. Or a hostile act occurs within the commanders area of interest.

qas.q: What is the overall SF objective for hostage/barricaded suspects?
qas.a: - Title 18, United States Code, US Constitution

qas.q: What is FPCON ALPHA?
qas.a: - AF Form 2586

qas.q: What is FPCON CHARLIE?
qas.a: - 1 escort per 8 individuals

qas.q: What authorizes the detention of civilians for offenses committed on a military installation? (3)
qas.a: - Name, rank, last 6 of SSN, organization, clearance and inclusive dates of travel.

qas.q: What Form is the Unescorted Entry Authorization Certificate?
qas.a: - BDOC

qas.q: Escort officials must be properly designated and remain within close proximity at all times. The maximum allowable on Dyess AFB is ______ Escort per ______ individuals.
qas.a: - Mission, enemy, terrain and weather, troops available, time available, and civil considerations.

qas.q: Authenticated crew orders can be used as an EAL for transient aircraft so long as they have what pertinent information?
qas.a: - Delaware gate, Maryland gate, Southwest contracting gate.

qas.q: Who is the approval authority for crossing the restricted area boundary ("breaking red")?
qas.a: - At least one armed SF or ID force member will be posted to control entry.

qas.q: What does METT-TC stand for?
qas.a: - 5 at a time

qas.q: What are the three special purpose gates on Dyess AFB?
qas.a: - CAC, Teslin Cards (ie: retired, dependent)

qas.q: When "special purpose" gates are opened, what are the security requirements?
qas.a: - DAFB FM 173

qas.q: Refer to next for base entry authorized credentials specifics:
qas.a: - No more than 72 hours

qas.q: Military/DoD card holders may sponsor up to how many personnel?
qas.a: - 30 days

qas.q: What two forms of ID can be registered into DBIDS?
qas.a: - PL3

qas.q: What form is used for as an application for a contractors badge?
qas.a: - Armory, CATM (Alt Armory), Army Reserve Center, and Marine Reserve Center

qas.q: How long will AF Form 75 visitors passes be valid for?
qas.a: - Action or attack that resulted in death or serious bodily injury.

qas.q: Visitors staying in base housing can receive a pass for up to how many days?
qas.a: - Moderate

qas.q: As a minimum all Command posts are designated what? (PL)
qas.a: - The individual security forces patrol

qas.q: Where are all of the locations you can arm up on base?
qas.a: - Mission impact, local policy, patrol vehicle's capability, risk of danger, weather, road conditions, time of day, facilities along the route, and vehicle characteristics.

qas.q: What is classified as a major offense?
qas.a: - Location, direction and speed of travel. Reason for pursuit. Vehicle description to include license and occupants. Traffic conditions.

qas.q: Patrols are permitted to use pursuit driving at __________________ speeds.
qas.a: - Covering the streets parallel to the one the pursuit is on, creating a "boxing in" effect.

qas.q: Who has the responsibility for making the decision to pursue?
qas.a: - 5 to 7 car lengths

qas.q: If pursuit driving is determined to be necessary, what factors must be considered?
qas.a: - Only when innocent bystanders will not be endangered

qas.q: What information must be relayed to BDOC when a pursuit begins?
qas.a: - One owner/user persons

qas.q: Patrols responding to assist in a pursuit should concentrate on what?
qas.a: - Two owner/user persons

qas.q: What is the safe distance that should be used during a pursuit?
qas.a: - Two owner/user persons and at least one armed SF member.

qas.q: When is it ok to use roadblocks?
qas.a: - Closed net

qas.q: Who transports funds less than $7,500 on or off base?
qas.a: - Aircraft rail numbers, arrival of special aircraft, post limitations, shift change/schedules, primary or Alt BDOC locations, names of victims or witnesses.

qas.q: Who transport funds between $7,500 and $25,000 on or off base?
qas.a: - 1A and 2A

qas.q: Who transport funds over $25,000 on or off base?
qas.a: - Whistle, flashlight, cold/foul weather gear, handcuffs w/key, DAF badge, CAC card, RAB badge, ear protection, Law enforcement equipment, Defensor Fortis Load Carrying System.

qas.q: What type of communication network does Dyess have?
qas.a: - Communicate with DOT for local road conditions. If needed, change speed limits and dictate road closures.

qas.q: What cannot be broadcast over the net?
qas.a:
- Type 1: All personnel report.
- Type 2: All personnel report to normal pre-arranged shifts.
- Type 3: Telephone recall only

qas.q: What is the primary and alternate radio frequencies?
qas.a: - One ESRT and two ISRT

qas.q: What are the minimum equipment posting requirements?
qas.a: - BDOC

qas.q: What is security forces roll during inclement weather conditions?
qas.a: - FC

qas.q: What are the types of unit recalls?
qas.a: - On outermost garment above the waist

qas.q: What are the security requirements for the PL3 area?
qas.a: - AF IMT 1109

qas.q: Who assigns security incident numbers?
qas.a:
- Sector 1: All areas East of 3rd street
- Sector 2: All areas West of 3rd street and South of Avenue C
- Sector 3: All areas West of 3rd street and North of Avenue C

qas.q: Who can terminate a security incident?
qas.a: - No less than 2,000 feet

qas.q: How must personnel wear their RAB while in the restricted area?
qas.a: - UXO

qas.q: What document is used when personnel are escorting others into the PL1/PL2 area?
What are the patrol sectors?
qas.a: - If subject is confirmed to have hemophilia, blood clotting disorders or any other extenuating circumstances.

qas.q: If the aircraft cannot be moved during a Bomb threat, what is the evacuation distance?
qas.a:
- Rule for Court Martial
- Rule 302 (b)
- Article 7 (b)

qas.q: If an unauthorized drone lands on the base, what will it be treated as?
qas.a: - 14 days

qas.q: What is the covered wagon hotline number?
qas.a:
- Green = authorized
- Yellow = driving status suspended
- Brown = not registered
- Red = several possible warnings

qas.q: When is the only time urine or breath will be tested instead of blood?
qas.a: - Injury or death, greater than $10,000 in damage, disabled vehicle, any unusual circumstances too complicated to explain.

qas.q: What gives SF the authority to apprehend individuals?
qas.a: - 72 hours

qas.q: If a member requests legal council, how long must you wait before re-approaching?
qas.a: - Oblique, double abreast, increased double abreast, back-up offset, single file.

qas.q: What does the DBIDS color coded mean?
qas.a: - Any area larger than wingtip to wingtip or 10 square feet.

qas.q: What determines if a traffic accident is classified as "major"?
qas.a: Police 1 and security 1

qas.q: How long does a person have to contact BDOC after being issued a 1408 for an abandoned vehicle?
qas.a: - AFI 31-101 and AFGSC Supplement

qas.q: During a high risk traffic stop, what are the 5 main vehicle positions used?
qas.a: - 2 times

qas.q: What constitutes as a major fuel spill?
qas.a: - The duress situation, alarms, radio net, phones, and status checks

qas.q: Which post will be equipped with a drone buster?
qas.a: - The installation map located in BDOC and in post SSIs

qas.q: Primary, Alternate, and Exercise duress words will be established IAW what?
qas.a:
- identify additional witnesses
- identify suspects/perpetrators
- eliminate suspects
- secure evidence

qas.q: How many times can a patrol mis-authenticate before Positive Duress procedures are implemented?
qas.a: A reasonable belief that a crime was committed within the jurisdictional boundaries of the installation, by the person being apprehended.

qas.q: During BDOC duress/relocation, Arnold gate will assume primary control of what until Alt BDOC is activated?
qas.a: - Individual's parent, guardian or sponsor

qas.q: Base sectors are identified where?
qas.a: - An additional SF member or Sponsor's First Sergeant

qas.q: What are are some more specific reasons for conducting an interview?
qas.a: - Make sure another cooperative sponsor is present. Make sure a second SF member witnesses the removal. If no other sponsor is available, stop questioning and contact SJA for further guidance.

qas.q: The decision to apprehend a military member on base must be based on what?
qas.a: - Involves violating misdemeanor categorized laws subject to state and federal codes.

qas.q: Who must be present for questioning while dealing with a juvenile?
qas.a: - An examination of a person, property or premises to uncover evidence of a crime or criminal intent, such as stolen goods, burglary tools and/or weapons.

qas.q: If you are not able to make contact with a juveniles parent, guardian or sponsor, who else may be present to witness rights advisement as well as questioning?
qas.a: - All areas and items within immediate control of the apprehended person

qas.q: If the parent, guardian, or sponsor interferes with the investigation of a juvenile, are you allowed to remove them? What must you ensure prior to removing them?
qas.a:
- Emergency or life threatening situations.
- Another SF member needs to be present

qas.q: Describe a minor incident
qas.a: - Anything illegal for an individual to posses

qas.q: Define Search
qas.a:
Area 3 = 7 BW/Command Post
Area 4 = 7 BW/Crisis Action Team (CAT)
Area 6 = Aircraft Mass Parking Area
Area 9 = Aircraft Generation Area
Area 12 = 7 MUNS/Munitions Storage Area

qas.q: SF personnel making an apprehension will immediately search all offenders. What areas/items will be searched?
qas.a: - The capitol letter E to the right of areas 1 through 10 and left of areas 11 through 20

qas.q: In what cases will an SF member be permitted to search a suspect of an opposite sex? Who needs to be present?
qas.a: - Outside of their vehicle

qas.q: Define Contraband
qas.a: - Master Restricted Area Badge Listing

qas.q: The AF FM 1199-D is blue and will only have what areas open?
qas.a:
- The card is an AF FM 1199-D
- The card is blue
- Badge NO. on front and card number on back, completes the word "DYESS"
- AFGSC crest over right shoulder and 7BW crest over left shoulder

qas.q: Personal designated as an Escort will have what displayed on their RAB?
qas.a:
- Personal recognition
- Personal signature check
- EALs
- Radio Verification with BDOC using MRABL

qas.q: When conducting ECP checks, ECs must be positioned where?
qas.a:
- 1 hour
- Base Ops

qas.q: ECs will radio BDOC to verify names and RA badges against what to verify personnel are authorized to enter?
qas.a: - During increased FPCONS and DEFCONS when the alert crew billets are designed as a restricted area.

qas.q: When checking the AF 1199-D, all ECs will physically inspect each badge to verify the following badge authenticators:
qas.a:
- S3 will shadow the aircraft to the north most edge of the restricted area, adjacent to the transient ramp
- S2 will remain static at the sector boundary

qas.q: In addition to local authenticator, what are some supporting techniques that can be used?
qas.a: - November 2 and 3 will post out. Security 1 will make contact with November 1 EC and issue two radios via AF FM 1297

qas.q: Security 1 will contact the Dyess tower __________ prior to transient aircraft arrival for authorization to conduct sweeps. After sweeps are completed, where will S1 standby.
qas.a:
- November 1 = next to Raven team
- November 2 = Close Boundary Sentry
- November 3 = Immediately outside restricted area

qas.q: November 4 serves as the Billeting Entry Controller and will only post when?
qas.a:
- two
- Areas 1 and 2

qas.q: Upon arrival of transient aircraft, what will security 2 & 3 accomplish?
qas.a: - 30 minutes

qas.q: Once a transient aircraft arrives and is now at its parking spot. What will S1 and the November teams do?
qas.a: - S1 will initiate a security incident utilizing a SALUTE report, as directed by FC or higher authority

qas.q: What are the post requirements for each November team when providing security for transient aircraft?
qas.a: - S1 will check facility and surrounding areas to determine if maintenance personnel are present. If maintenance personnel cannot be located, S1 will contact the appropriate MOC to determine who should be in the facility

qas.q: While transient aircraft remains at DAFB. Security 1 will perform ______ checks per shift of which Clandestine areas?
qas.a: - It depends on who has security of the flight line/facilities (SF or Maintenance personnel)

qas.q: November 2 and 3 will remain in place how many minutes after aircraft departure?
qas.a: - U.S. military aircraft, military personnel, and military cargo.

qas.q: If "SF" has responsibility of security of flight line facilities containing PL resources found unsecured, what will Security 1 accomplish?
qas.a: - Bldg 4218 (primary) and Bldg 4225 (alternate)

qas.q: If "Maintenance personnel" has responsibility of security of flight line facilities containing PL resources found unsecured, what will Security 1 accomplish?
qas.a: - Police 1

qas.q: The decision to initiate a security incident for unsecured flight line facilities depends on what?
qas.a: - During periods of increased FPCON or as directed by FC, DFC or higher authority

qas.q: The 7 SFS Military Customs Inspector will only conduct customs inspections on:
qas.a: - During the school year from 0730-0830 and 1430-1600 as directed by S30 or higher authority

qas.q: Where are the Customs Inspection holding areas located?
qas.a: - Gate closures due to holidays

qas.q: Who shall act as supervisor for all other police units and gates?
qas.a:
- 10 mph
- Flight chief

qas.q: When would additional installation patrols be posted (MWD, foot, bicycle, etc)?
qas.a: - FC or higher authority

qas.q: When will Delaware gate be opened? Directed by whom?
qas.a: - That they understand and agree on route of travel

qas.q: The monthly Delaware Gate roster can be used to find what specific information?
qas.a: - The patrol will make sure the vehicle is parked in a central location, the vehicle is secured, and all equipment is placed out of plain view

qas.q: During a code 3 response, patrols may not exceed ____________ over the speed limit unless directed otherwise by _______.
qas.a: - To make sure vehicles, material, and personnel flow safely and efficiently on DAFB roadways

qas.q: Who is the final approval authority to release traffic at the gates following an alarm response?
qas.a: - Dyess Instruction 31-218, Motor Vehicle Traffic Supervision

qas.q: What must SF ensure the custodian understands, during a funds escort, prior to beginning the escort?
qas.a: - Anytime an illegal or potentially hazardous act is detected

qas.q: When a patrol initiates a foot patrol, what must they accomplish beforehand?
qas.a: - Location, initiation time, termination time and results

qas.q: What is SF main goal/priority?
qas.a: - False

qas.q: Specific guidance for traffic enforcement can be found where?
qas.a:
- Aid the injured
- Protect the scene
- Investigate the accident
- Record names and info of personnel involved

qas.q: When will traffic enforcement actions be taken?
qas.a: - Accidents which do not meet the requirements for a major vehicle accident

qas.q: Whenever an installation patrol is conducting selective traffic enforcement, they must inform BDOC of what?
qas.a: - That the accident does not hinder normal traffic flow

qas.q: True or False: Patrols are allowed to mix moving and non moving violations when issuing traffic citations.
qas.a:
- 72 hours
- S2I

qas.q: What are the primary responsibilities (4) of SF personnel at an accident scene?
qas.a: - Report the citation to their sponsor within 24 hours

qas.q: Define minor vehicle accident:
qas.a: - Individuals First sergeant, Commander or E-7 and above as directed by their commander

qas.q: For minor accidents in a roadway, BDOC will dispatch police units to ensure what?
qas.a: - The sponsor's unit commander/first sergeant and the base family advocacy officer

qas.q: For abandoned vehicles, the individual has ___________ to contact BDOC after given a citation. Who will the notice also be forwarded to?
qas.a: - Two in the front door and One at the back door

qas.q: When issuing a ticket to civilians utilizing a DD FM 1408, what shall you instruct them to do?
qas.a: - 30 seconds

qas.q: Upon termination of a shoplifting incident involving a military member, whom will they be released to?
qas.a: - True

qas.q: Who must be advised of all incidents of family violence?
qas.a: - The check will be stopped, the operator and occupants will be detained, and the vehicle will be secured.

qas.q: When responding to domestic situations, assuming you have multiple patrols, how many and where should patrols be positioned?
qas.a: - Military members and DoD Civilians

qas.q: When conducting one leg stand test, how long does the violator need to be able to hold there stand?
qas.a: - Police 1 and Security 1

qas.q: True of False: Under the Implied Consent Policy, an individual does NOT have the right to consult an attorney before deciding to submit or refuse a test of their blood.
qas.a: - UXO and EOD will be contacted

qas.q: If during a RIEVC, unauthorized weapons, drug contraband, or government property is discovered, what will be the SF members next course of action?
qas.a: - FOUO

qas.q: Which individuals DO NOT have the right to refuse to submit to a RIEVC?
qas.a: - Every six months or when compromised

qas.q: Which two posts will be posted with a drone buster at all times?
qas.a:
- Unit Recall (everybody reports ASAP
- Unit Alert (Every either report or be on 6 ring)
- Unit Accountability (completed telephone pyramid)

qas.q: If a drone is engaged and brought down on Dyess AFB property, the crash site will be treated as what? Who will be contacted?
qas.a:
- Security 2 & 3 will proceed to western restricted area boundary and hold until A/C turns on taxiway Alpha. They will shadow the aircraft to ensure it doesn't turn into the restricted area.

- Security 1 will take up position at the nose of the A/C and signal for them to shut down engines. All personnel attempting to exit before approval from SF leadership will be challenged

qas.q: What is the classification of duress words?
qas.a: - Incident Commander

qas.q: When are duress words changed?
qas.a: - To develop a cordon out of range of weapons fire, to protect bystanders, and to keep nonessential personnel out of the area

qas.q: What are the 3 types of Unit Recalls?
qas.a: - Near the outer zone, out of suspects sight, with adequate parking spaces, telephones and protection from gunfire

qas.q: How will security units respond for an unannounced aircraft arrival?
qas.a: - Cut of phones, heat, air conditioning, lights and water

qas.q: Who establishes an inner security zone?
qas.a: - Gloves, gowns, aprons, face shield masks, booties, eye protection, and resuscitation bags

qas.q: What is the purpose of an outer zone?
qas.a: - 4th Amendment
